title: peucedanum terebinthaceum var. paishanense
intro: Peucedanum terebinthaceum var. paishanense, also known as Paishanese Tansy Fern, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to certain parts of Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic activities, and is used to treat a range of health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, as well as to aid in digestion and reduce menstrual cramps. In herbal preparations, Paishanese Tansy Fern can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its health benefits.
